steps.title: Identify what the word must mean in context
steps.content: The sun has already set, yet it is described as "manifest in the livid tongues of fire darting towards the zenith." The sun is not directly visible, but it can still be perceived through the fire-colored streaks it leaves in the sky. The word "manifest" therefore must mean something like able to be perceived or noticed.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Dwindling’: means gradually decreasing. While the fire-light is fading, "manifest" describes how the sun appears, not its rate of decrease — wrong sense entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Realized’: means understood or achieved. This would imply the sun is being comprehended mentally, but the passage describes a physical, sensory phenomenon — not a conceptual one. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Situated’: means located or placed. Describes physical position, not the act of being seen or sensed through the fire-colored light. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Perceptible’: means able to be perceived or noticed. The sun, though below the horizon, is still perceptible through the livid tongues of fire it leaves in the sky — a perfect fit. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
